visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a static shot of a vibrant stage backdrop featuring stylized drawings of musical instruments and characters. A full band, including a drummer and guitarists, is positioned on the stage, which is illuminated by bright overhead lights. Bold, colorful graphic text overlays the center of the frame. (2) A main character wearing a white t-shirt with a fruit pattern holds a microphone and a commemorative medal while laughing heartily. He stands on an outdoor stage under clear daylight, with multiple onlookers and other staff visible in the background against a large event banner with various logos. (3) The scene transitions into a rapid sequence of medium shots and close-ups of different male band members. One man in a grey sweatshirt speaks directly to the camera with a playful expression, while another in a cream-colored hoodie plays an electric guitar with high intensity and focus. (4) The final segment showcases a night performance on a large-scale outdoor concert stage. Beams of artificial light cut through the dark, and a massive LED screen displays dynamic visuals. The band members are seen in medium-full shots, actively performing their instruments for a large audience.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Slogan]: 只花了十萬塊包物超所值的. [Logo]: 七月半. (2) [Variety]: 活動站台. (3) [Variety]: 自己打歌. (4) [Variety]: 路跑變演唱會. (5) [Subtitle]: 七月半今年還需要貼小抄嗎？. (6) [Subtitle]: 今年不用貼了啊. (7) [Variety]: 因為直接用放的. (8) [Subtitle]: 今年還要再貼小抄嗎？. (9) [Subtitle]: 不用了不用了. (10) [Variety]: 但旁邊的要問一下. (11) [Subtitle]: 今年還要再貼小抄嗎？. (12) [Subtitle]: 一定會. (13) [Variety]: 今晚一起High翻台中!!!

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 3 main band members, 3 stage crew/performers in the background
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: The main characters include Tsai A-ga in a white fruit-patterned shirt and black shorts, HowHow in a cream hoodie and black hat, and Tsai Ge in a grey vest and black pants.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Outdoor festival stage with colorful graphics and logos for the 'Haha Baby Run' event during the day; a large-scale outdoor concert stage with dark surroundings and light beams at night.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-energy pop-rock music with a prominent drum beat and bright electric guitar distortion starts immediately. A male voice with a high arousal level and a friendly, energetic tone greets the audience. The acoustic environment has the characteristic reverb of a professional broadcast or large outdoor space. (2) A series of sharp post-production whoosh and cartoon-style sound effects mark transitions between quick dialogue clips. Multiple male voices are heard engaging in rapid-fire, humorous banter. The speech rate is fast, with high pitch variability indicating a lighthearted, comedic mood during the interview segments. (3) The background music continues with a steady tempo of approximately 130 BPM, featuring a strong bass line and clear percussive transients. The vocal clarity remains high as the main speaker continues to narrate and interact. The sound of a crowd laughing and clapping is subtly mixed in the background. (4) In the final phase, the audio captures a live concert atmosphere with increased spectral brightness and a denser musical arrangement. The vocals shift into singing, maintaining a joyful and positive emotional valence. The track concludes with a final crescendo of music and synthesized cheering sound effects for emphasis.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Montage: Hook -> Humorous Dialogue -> Concert Performance Highlights
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To promote the band Seven Months Half and their participation in the 2025 Haha Baby Run event in Taichung, utilizing humorous backstage banter and energetic concert footage to engage the audience.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Prominent display of 'Haha Baby Run' and 'momo' branding on the stage backdrop and within text overlays.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively bridges digital social interaction with physical event highlights, emphasizing the band's personality and their performance energy.
